title: Mbappé, the French team's adored and criticized superstar
subhed: The captain of Les Bleus, who begin their quest for a third World Cup title on Tuesday, boasts impressive statistics for both club and national. However, in recent years, his public image has declined.

Hungarian media landscape upended after Viktor Orban's fall from power
subhed: After many Hungarian media outlets were turned into propaganda machines under the previous government, the new prime minister, Péter Magyar, is moving swiftly to overhaul public broadcasting conglomerate MTVA, which includes the national news agency MTI.

title: Amid the ruins of Southern Lebanon, Hezbollah declares victory over Israel
subhed: Thousands of Lebanese civilians who had fled the fighting have begun returning to villages south of the Litani River, which were heavily bombarded over the 105 days of war. The Shiite armed group and political party is feeling emboldened, both in relation to the Lebanese state and to Israel, by the memorandum of understanding reached between Washington and Tehran.

title: Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' aliens have nothing to do with Trump's
subhed: Days before the film was released worldwide, the White House co-opted the imagery of 'aliens' to further its rhetoric and anti-immigration policy.

title: In China's Qinhuangdao, architect Moshe Safdie's futuristic complex reimagines urban living
subhed: The utopian 'Hai Bi Tai,' a 16-story residential complex with 1,800 units, most of which offer views of the sky, the city and the Bohai Sea, still has half its apartments standing empty due to a real estate crisis.

title: After the US-Iran deal, Israel revels in its military might but finds itself at a strategic impasse
subhed: While Israel aimed to eliminate the 'existential threat' posed by the Iranian regime, it was sidelined from the memorandum of understanding reached Sunday between the US and Iran. Meanwhile, its military now risks losing its freedom of action in Lebanon.
